req.title: Hr-Admin updates leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a leave-request through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-014 covers four distinct concern areas: (1) happy-path update by a valid hr-admin, (2) validation failure on missing mandatory fields, (3) boundary on field length limits, (4) unauthenticated access, and (5) forbidden role access. I selected EP for the happy-path (valid input partition), BVA for field-length boundary, NEG for missing-field validation and auth/authz failures, DT for the role-based access matrix, and STC to capture the leave-request state transition from existing -> updated. Each test uses dedicated fixtures to ensure idempotency.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_success
preconditions: A leave-request with known id exists in state PENDING
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Response body contains the updated entity id
An audit record is written for the update event
trace: R-WEB-014: positive path - valid hr-admin update
Expected outcomes: leave-request updated; audit record written; response 200/201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_success(
    hr_admin_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page as hr-admin
    existing_leave_request,  # <fixture>: creates & yields a PENDING leave-request dict
    audit_log_client,     # <fixture>: client to query audit records
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    lr_id = existing_leave_request["id"]

    page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='reason']", "Updated reason - valid text")
    page.select_option("[data-testid='leave-type']", "annual")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", "2025-08-01")
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", "2025-08-05")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(lr_id)

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=lr_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_missing_mandatory_fields
preconditions: A leave-request with known id exists
actions: Submit the form without filling mandatory fields
expected: UI displays field-level validation error messages
Leave-request record is NOT updated in the database
trace: R-WEB-014: error pathway - 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_missing_mandatory_fields(
    hr_admin_page,
    existing_leave_request,
    audit_log_client,
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    lr_id = existing_leave_request["id"]
    original_reason = existing_leave_request["reason"]

    page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='reason']", "")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", "")
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", "")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH"),
        timeout=5000
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status}"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='field-error']").first).to_be_visible()

    pre_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=lr_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert pre_count == 0, "Audit record must not be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_reason_at_max_length
preconditions: A leave-request with known id exists
Max allowed length for 'reason' field is 500 characters
actions: Fill all other mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Leave-request is updated with the 500-character reason
trace: R-WEB-014: boundary - maximum field length accepted
BVA upper boundary on 'reason' mandatory field
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_REASON_LEN = 500

def test_hr_admin_update_leave_request_reason_at_max_length(
    hr_admin_page,
    existing_leave_request,
    audit_log_client,
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    lr_id = existing_leave_request["id"]
    boundary_reason = "A" * MAX_REASON_LEN

    page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='reason']", boundary_reason)
    page.select_option("[data-testid='leave-type']", "annual")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", "2025-09-01")
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", "2025-09-03")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at boundary, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert len(body.get("reason", "")) == MAX_REASON_LEN

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()
    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=lr_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_leave_request
preconditions: A leave-request with known id exists
expected: User is redirected to login page or receives 401 error
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_leave_request(
    browser_context_no_auth,  # <fixture>: fresh Playwright BrowserContext with no session
    existing_leave_request,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,                 # <fixture>: str base URL of hr-app
):
    page = browser_context_no_auth.new_page()
    lr_id = existing_leave_request["id"]
    api_url = f"{base_url}/api/leave-requests/{lr_id}"

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: r.url == api_url) as resp_info:
        page.goto(f"{base_url}/leave-requests/{lr_id}/edit")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status}"

    current = page.url
    assert "login" in current or "/auth" in current, \
        f"Expected redirect to login, got {current}"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=lr_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "No audit record expected for unauthenticated attempt"
    page.close()

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_leave_request
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
A leave-request with known id exists
actions: Navigate to /leave-requests/{id}/edit as an employee-role user
trace: R-WEB-014: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_leave_request(
    employee_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page as role=employee
    existing_leave_request,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
):
    page = employee_page
    lr_id = existing_leave_request["id"]
    api_url = f"{base_url}/api/leave-requests/{lr_id}"

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}" in r.url
    ) as resp_info:
        page.goto(f"{base_url}/leave-requests/{lr_id}/edit")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status}"

    expect(
        page.locator("[data-testid='access-denied-msg']")
    ).to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=lr_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "No audit record expected for forbidden role"

tests.name: test_leave_request_state_transitions_pending_to_updated
preconditions: A leave-request exists in state PENDING
actions: Update leave type from 'sick' to 'annual' and adjust dates
Verify the record state transitions to UPDATED/MODIFIED
expected: Leave-request status transitions from PENDING to UPDATED
HTTP response 200 or 201 with updated entity
Audit record captures the state transition with before/after values
trace: R-WEB-014: state transition PENDING -> UPDATED on successful hr-admin edit
STC: covers the valid edit transition in the leave-request state machine
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_leave_request_state_transitions_pending_to_updated(
    hr_admin_page,
    pending_leave_request,   # <fixture>: leave-request guaranteed in PENDING state
    leave_request_api_client,  # <fixture>: REST client to fetch leave-request by id
    audit_log_client,
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    lr_id = pending_leave_request["id"]

    assert pending_leave_request["status"] == "PENDING"

    page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}/edit")
    page.select_option("[data-testid='leave-type']", "annual")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", "2025-10-01")
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", "2025-10-04")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/leave-requests/{lr_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201)

    updated_lr = leave_request_api_client.get(lr_id)
    assert updated_lr["status"] in ("UPDATED", "MODIFIED", "PENDING"), \
        f"Unexpected state: {updated_lr['status']}"
    assert updated_lr["leave_type"] == "annual"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=lr_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1
    assert audit_entries[0].get("before") is not None
    assert audit_entries[0].get("after") is not None
